title: Symbol Model
description: A code graph needs a unified, language-agnostic representation of source code structure before any persistence, traversal, or impact analysis can operate on it. The symbol model defines the domain entities and value objects that represent files, symbols, and the relationships between them — the foundational vocabulary for the entire `@specd/code-graph` package.
context: A code graph needs a unified, language-agnostic representation of source code structure before any persistence, traversal, or impact analysis can operate on it. The symbol model defines the domain entities and value objects that represent files, symbols, and the relationships between them — the foundational vocabulary for the entire `@specd/code-graph` package.

rules:
A `FileNode` SHALL represent a single source file in the workspace. It contains:

- **`path`** (`string`) — globally unique path in format `{workspaceName}:{relativeToCodeRoot}` (e.g. `core:src/index.ts`). Forward-slash-normalized. This is the node's canonical identity.
- **`configRelativePath`** (`string`) — forward-slash-normalized path from the directory containing the `specd.yaml` used for indexing to the file on disk (e.g. `packages/core/src/index.ts`). This path is not the node identity and MAY include `..` segments when the file lives outside the config directory.
- **`language`** (`string`) — language identifier (e.g. `typescript`, `python`). Determined by the language adapter at index time.
- **`contentHash`** (`string`) — hash of the file's content at last index. Used for incremental diffing.
- **`workspace`** (`string`) — the workspace name this file belongs to (e.g. `core`, `cli`, `default`).
- **`embedding`** (`Float32Array | undefined`) — optional vector embedding for semantic search. Deferred to v2+.

Two `FileNode` values are equal if and only if their `path` fields match (since `path` includes the workspace name prefix and remains the canonical graph identity). `configRelativePath` exists for user-facing lookup and diagnostics; it MUST NOT replace the canonical workspace-prefixed path in persisted relations or symbol identifiers.

A `SpecNode` SHALL represent a spec directory in the workspace. It contains:

- **`specId`** (`string`) — the spec identifier in `workspace:package/topic` format (e.g. `core:change`, `_global:_global/architecture`). This is the node's identity.
- **`path`** (`string`) — workspace-relative path to the spec directory (e.g. `specs/core/change`).
- **`title`** (`string`) — from `.specd-metadata.yaml` `title` field. Defaults to the `specId` if metadata is absent.
- **`description`** (`string`) — from `.specd-metadata.yaml` `description` field. Empty string if absent.
- **`contentHash`** (`string`) — hash of concatenated spec artifacts, excluding `.specd-metadata.yaml`. `spec.md` first if present, then remaining artifacts in alphabetical order. Used for incremental diffing.
- **`content`** (`string`) — concatenated artifact text for full-text search. Same ordering as `contentHash`: `spec.md` first if present, then remaining artifacts alphabetically. Excludes `.specd-metadata.yaml`.
- **`dependsOn`** (`string[]`) — ordered list of spec IDs this spec depends on, extracted from `.specd-metadata.yaml`. Defaults to `[]` if metadata is absent.
- **`workspace`** (`string`) — the workspace name this spec belongs to (e.g. `core`, `_global`).

Two `SpecNode` values are equal if their `specId` fields match.

A `SymbolNode` SHALL represent a named code construct extracted from a file. It contains:

- **`id`** (`string`) — deterministic identifier computed from `filePath + kind + name + line + column` (e.g. `core:src/index.ts:function:main:1:0`). Since `filePath` is workspace-prefixed, the id is globally unique across workspaces. The same symbol at the same location always produces the same id.
- **`name`** (`string`) — the symbol's declared name.
- **`kind`** (`SymbolKind`) — the category of this symbol.
- **`filePath`** (`string`) — workspace-prefixed path of the file containing this symbol.
- **`line`** (`number`) — 1-based line number of the symbol's declaration.
- **`column`** (`number`) — 0-based column offset of the symbol's declaration.
- **`comment`** (`string | undefined`) — the raw comment text.

All node families (Specs, Files, Symbols, Documents) SHALL use workspace-qualified canonical identities as their unique primary keys.

The code graph SHALL define immutable runtime analysis value objects that capture the complete compact result of analyzing a source file before relation resolution.

The shared runtime model MUST include:

- **`FileAnalysisDraft`** — adapter-emitted file analysis before the indexer assigns run-local file identity
- **`FileAnalysis`** — registered file analysis that includes the file's run-local ID, workspace-prefixed path, language, content hash, compact facts, and optional per-file parser state
- **`ParserState`** — optional compact parser-specific state retained only when it avoids repeated deterministic work later in the same indexing run
- **`AdapterSessionState`** — optional compact run-scoped adapter state retained only when it provides deterministic shared caches across multiple files in the same indexing run

`FileAnalysisDraft` and `FileAnalysis` MUST represent, at minimum:

- symbols emitted for the file
- import declarations emitted for the file
- deterministic binding facts emitted for the file
- deterministic call facts emitted for the file
- namespace or equivalent qualified-name context when the language supports it
- optional file/document node metadata already normalized for indexing

These runtime analysis value objects SHALL remain distinct from persisted graph-store entities. They exist to support in-memory indexing passes and MUST NOT require changes to persisted symbol IDs, relation IDs, or graph-store schemas.

The symbol model SHALL support run-scoped lookup structures that make deterministic relation resolution bounded by relevant matches rather than full symbol scans.

The shared runtime lookup model MUST support:

- file lookup by workspace-prefixed path and run-local file ID
- symbol lookup by file, simple name, and qualified name
- traversal from file to symbols and symbol back to file
- lookup of specs and documents that participate in cross-entity relations during the same indexing run
- traversal between specs and covered symbols when that relationship is needed by later phases
- relation deduplication keys before final persistence

These lookups MAY be implemented by `IndexSession` and `SymbolIndex` collaborating internally, but the externally visible runtime model MUST preserve a common lookup contract across all built-in adapters and indexer phases.

The retained runtime analysis model MUST prioritize compact, immutable data over heavyweight parser artifacts to reduce garbage-collection pressure during large indexing runs.

Retained `ParserState`, `AdapterSessionState`, binding facts, call facts, imports, and lookup indexes MUST use plain data structures and MUST NOT retain AST nodes, parser trees, parser cursors, or equivalent heavyweight objects once per-file analysis completes.

The graph SHALL support a `document` category for textual non-code nodes.

- **`DocumentNode`** represents a file that does not map to symbol semantics but is searchable via full-text content and identifiable by path.
- Identities for documents follow the same format as `FileNode` (`workspace:path`), including the reserved `root:` namespace.

`SymbolKind` SHALL be a closed string enum with the following members:

- `function`
- `class`
- `method`
- `variable`
- `type`
- `interface`
- `enum`

No other values are permitted. Language adapters MUST map language-specific constructs to one of these kinds or skip the construct entirely.

Relations between nodes SHALL be represented as typed edges. Each relation has a `source`, `target`, and `type`. The following relation types are defined:

| Type            | Source | Target | Meaning                                                               | Phase |
| --------------- | ------ | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----- |
| `IMPORTS`       | File   | File   | Source file imports from target file                                  | v1.5  |
| `DEFINES`       | File   | Symbol | File contains the symbol's declaration                                | v2    |
| `CALLS`         | Symbol | Symbol | Source symbol invokes target symbol                                   | v2    |
| `CONSTRUCTS`    | Symbol | Symbol | Source symbol constructs or instantiates target type                  | v2    |
| `USES_TYPE`     | Symbol | Symbol | Source symbol references target type in a static signature or binding | v2    |
| `EXPORTS`       | File   | Symbol | File exports the symbol as public API                                 | v2    |
| `DEPENDS_ON`    | Spec   | Spec   | Spec depends on target spec                                           | v1.5  |
| `COVERS_FILE`   | Spec   | File   | Spec has a file-level implementation link to the target file          | v2+   |
| `COVERS_SYMBOL` | Spec   | Symbol | Spec has a symbol-level implementation link to the target symbol      | v2+   |
| `EXTENDS`       | Symbol | Symbol | Source type extends or inherits from target type                      | v2    |
| `IMPLEMENTS`    | Symbol | Symbol | Source type fulfills or implements target contract/type               | v2    |
| `OVERRIDES`     | Symbol | Symbol | Source method overrides or concretely fulfills target one             | v2    |

Relations MAY include a `stale` boolean flag in their `metadata`.

For implementation traceability, `stale` applies specifically to `COVERS_SYMBOL` relations whose target symbol is no longer detectable in the current graph index. It is not the generic marker for workspace-boundary or archive-materialization failures.

A `Relation` SHALL be a value object containing:

- **`source`** (`string`) — the id or path of the source node.
- **`target`** (`string`) — the id or path of the target node.
- **`type`** (`RelationType`) — one of the relation types defined above.
- **`metadata`** (`Record<string, unknown> | undefined`) — optional adapter-specific metadata (e.g. import specifier, call site line number).

Two `Relation` values are equal if `source`, `target`, and `type` all match.

Hierarchy relations SHALL use the following normalization rules:

- `EXTENDS` connects one type symbol to another type symbol when the source inherits behavior or structure from the target.
- `IMPLEMENTS` connects one type symbol to another contract-bearing symbol when the source fulfills the target's required shape or behavior.
- `OVERRIDES` connects one method symbol to another method symbol when the source method replaces, concretizes, or fulfills the target method through inheritance or contract implementation.

`OVERRIDES` is a first-class persisted relation in this model; it is not deferred to derived traversal logic.

Adapters MAY normalize inheritance-adjacent constructs from supported languages into these relations when the normalized edge remains useful for code understanding, impact discovery, hotspot analysis, and affected-spec discovery.

An `ImportDeclaration` SHALL represent a parsed import statement or deterministic import expression extracted from source code. It contains:

- **`localName`** (`string`) — the identifier used locally in the importing file. File-only import forms use an empty string.
- **`originalName`** (`string`) — the identifier as declared in the source module. File-only import forms use an empty string.
- **`specifier`** (`string`) — the raw import specifier string.
- **`isRelative`** (`boolean`) — true if the specifier is a relative path.
- **`kind`** (`'named' | 'namespace' | 'default' | 'side-effect' | 'dynamic' | 'require' | 'blank' | undefined`) — optional import form metadata. `undefined` is equivalent to `named` for backwards compatibility.

`ImportDeclaration` is a pure syntactic representation — it contains no resolution information. The indexer resolves specifiers to files and symbol ids using the adapter registry and monorepo package map.

Side-effect imports, string-literal dynamic imports, CommonJS `require()` calls, and Go blank imports MUST be representable as file-only import declarations without creating fake local symbols.

The symbol model SHALL define immutable, language-agnostic value objects for deterministic scoped binding and call resolution. These value objects are analysis inputs and intermediate results that normalize source-language facts before they are converted into persisted graph relations.

The shared model SHALL represent:

- **Scope ownership**: file, class/type, method, function, and block scopes when the adapter can identify them deterministically.
- **Binding facts**: source-location-aware facts that bind a visible name or receiver to a declared, constructed, imported, inherited, framework-managed, or otherwise deterministic target type or symbol candidate.
- **Binding source kind**: local, parameter, property/field, class-managed, inherited, file/global, imported type, framework-managed, constructor call, and alias-from-known-binding.
- **Call facts**: source-location-aware facts for free calls, member calls, static calls, and constructor calls, including the enclosing caller symbol when known.
- **Resolution metadata**: deterministic reason strings and optional confidence or diagnostic metadata that explain why a binding or call candidate was accepted or dropped.

Binding facts and call facts MUST be immutable value objects with `readonly` properties. They MUST use workspace-prefixed file paths and symbol IDs when they reference graph entities.

**Cycle Detection**: The scoped binding environment resolution SHALL implement cycle detection when traversing the scope hierarchy (e.g., following `parentId` links). If a circular reference is detected, the traversal MUST be aborted to prevent infinite loops, and no resolution result SHALL be returned for that lookup.

The first iteration SHALL remain conservative. It MUST NOT model arbitrary runtime state, reflection, runtime container identifiers, interprocedural propagation, or whole-program data flow.

Scoped binding and call resolution SHALL emit deterministic graph relations using the persisted relation vocabulary:

- `IMPORTS` for file-level dependencies that resolve to files.
- `CALLS` for deterministic invocations from one symbol to another callable symbol.
- `CONSTRUCTS` for deterministic constructor or constructor-like instantiation from an enclosing symbol to the constructed class/type symbol.
- `USES_TYPE` for deterministic type references from an enclosing symbol to a referenced type, interface, enum, or class symbol.
- `EXTENDS`, `IMPLEMENTS`, and `OVERRIDES` for deterministic hierarchy and method-fulfillment relationships.

Constructor injection, typed parameters, typed return values, typed properties/fields, and imported type annotations SHALL emit `USES_TYPE` when the referenced type symbol resolves deterministically.

Constructor calls such as `new ClassName()` and language-equivalent constructor-like expressions SHALL emit `CONSTRUCTS` when the constructed target resolves deterministically.

Scoped binding and call resolution SHALL NOT persist symbol-to-symbol dependency relations where the source symbol id and target symbol id are identical. Self-relations do not represent external blast radius and MUST be dropped before graph persistence.

Unresolved binding facts and call facts SHALL NOT create speculative graph edges. They MAY be preserved as non-persisted diagnostics or debug metadata for tests and troubleshooting.

All model types — `FileNode`, `SymbolNode`, `Relation`, and any aggregate containing them — SHALL be immutable value objects. Properties MUST be `readonly`. Mutations to the graph happen exclusively through the `GraphStore` port's atomic upsert operations, which replace entire file-scoped slices. There is no in-place mutation API.

`@specd/code-graph` SHALL define its own `SpecdCodeGraphError` base class which MUST extend `SpecdError` from `@specd/core`. All package-specific errors MUST extend `SpecdCodeGraphError`.

By extending `SpecdError`, all code-graph errors automatically follow the "Specd Error Contract" defined in [`default:_global/error-handling-conventions`](../../_global/error-handling-conventions/spec.md), including the `specd: true` discriminator and machine-readable `code` requirement.

- `InvalidSymbolKindError` — when a symbol kind value is not in the `SymbolKind` enum
- `InvalidRelationTypeError` — when a relation type value is not in the defined set
- `DuplicateSymbolIdError` — when two symbols in the same file produce the same deterministic id
- `StoreNotOpenError` — when an operation is attempted on a closed or uninitialized store

constraints: All model types are value objects — no identity beyond structural equality (except SymbolNode.id which is deterministic)
SymbolKind is a closed enum — adding a new kind requires a spec change
RelationType is a closed set — adding a new type requires a spec change
Depends on @specd/core as a runtime dependency
embedding on FileNode is deferred to v2+ — adapters MUST NOT populate it until the embedding pipeline is implemented
COVERS relations are deferred to v2+ — the type exists in the model but is not populated by any v1.5/v2 process
DEPENDS_ON relations are populated by the indexer in v1.5, reading from .specd-metadata.yaml or spec.md
CONSTRUCTS and USES_TYPE are first-class persisted relations in v2 and are part of the base graph vocabulary for supported languages
EXTENDS, IMPLEMENTS, and OVERRIDES are first-class persisted relations in v2 and are part of the base graph vocabulary for supported languages

scenarios.name: FileNode equality still uses canonical path
given: two FileNode values with the same canonical path but different contentHash and configRelativePath
then: they are considered equal because path remains the canonical graph identity

scenarios.name: FileNode path normalization
given: a file discovered under workspace core at OS path src\\domain\\entities\\change.ts
then: path is stored as core:src/domain/entities/change.ts

scenarios.name: FileNode configRelativePath is repository-style
given: the active specd.yaml lives at /project/specd.yaml
AND workspace core has codeRoot /project/packages/core
AND the discovered file is /project/packages/core/src/index.ts
then: path is core:src/index.ts
AND configRelativePath is packages/core/src/index.ts
AND workspace is core

scenarios.name: FileNode configRelativePath may include parent segments
given: the active specd.yaml lives at /project/apps/web/specd.yaml
AND workspace core has codeRoot /project/packages/core
AND the discovered file is /project/packages/core/src/index.ts
then: configRelativePath is ../../packages/core/src/index.ts
AND it does not replace the canonical path

scenarios.name: SpecNode equality by specId
given: two SpecNode values with the same specId but different title
then: they are considered equal

scenarios.name: dependsOn extracted from metadata
given: a spec directory with .specd-metadata.yaml containing dependsOn: [core:config, core:storage]
then: dependsOn is ['core:config', 'core:storage']

scenarios.name: SpecNode includes workspace field
given: a spec discovered in workspace core
then: workspace is 'core'

scenarios.name: SpecNode dependsOn defaults to empty
given: a spec with no dependencies
when: a SpecNode is created without dependsOn
then: dependsOn defaults to []

scenarios.name: Deterministic id generation
given: a symbol with filePath: 'core/src/utils.ts', kind: function, name: 'hash', line: 10, column: 0
then: it produces 'core/src/utils.ts:function:hash:10:0' every time for these inputs

scenarios.name: Different line produces different id
given: two symbols with the same filePath, kind, and name but different line values
then: the ids are different

scenarios.name: Id includes workspace-prefixed path
given: a symbol in file core:src/index.ts (workspace-prefixed path)
then: the id starts with core:src/index.ts:
AND the id is globally unique across workspaces

scenarios.name: Comment extracted from JSDoc
given: a TypeScript file with /** Computes the hash. */ immediately before function computeHash()
then: comment is '/** Computes the hash. */'

scenarios.name: Symbol without comment
given: a function declaration with no preceding comment
then: comment is undefined

scenarios.name: Registered file analysis retains compact normalized facts
given: an adapter has analyzed a file containing symbols, imports, binding facts, and call facts
when: the indexer registers the result as FileAnalysis
then: the runtime model records the file ID, workspace-prefixed path, language, content hash, and those compact facts together
AND optional namespace or parser state is retained only when present

scenarios.name: File analysis remains separate from persisted graph entities
given: a FileAnalysis has been registered in memory
then: persisted graph symbols and relations are derived from that analysis
AND the runtime analysis object itself is not persisted as a graph-store entity

scenarios.name: Relation resolution uses bounded common lookups
given: the session has registered files and symbols from multiple workspaces
when: Pass 2 resolves a name, qualified name, or file target
then: it uses the common lookup structures for file, symbol, and qualified-name access
AND it does not require a full scan of all registered symbols for every lookup

scenarios.name: Cross-entity lookups include specs and covered symbols
given: the session has registered specs and their covered symbols during the same indexing run
when: a later phase asks for symbols covered by a spec or specs covering a symbol
then: the runtime lookup model answers through the shared cross-entity indexes
AND it does not recompute the mapping from persisted relations on demand

scenarios.name: Relation deduplication happens before persistence
given: multiple deterministic resolution paths derive the same relation
when: those relations are added to the run-scoped lookup model
then: they collapse to one deduplicated relation before bulk loading

scenarios.name: Parser-specific retained state excludes heavyweight parser objects
given: an adapter stores optional parser-specific state for later deterministic resolution
when: retained runtime analysis state is inspected after Pass 1
then: that state uses plain compact data structures
AND it excludes AST nodes, parser trees, parser cursors, and equivalent heavyweight parser artifacts

scenarios.name: Run-scoped adapter cache state also excludes heavyweight parser objects
given: an adapter stores optional run-scoped cache state shared across multiple files
when: that retained state is inspected after Pass 1
then: it uses plain compact data structures
AND it excludes AST nodes, parser trees, parser cursors, and equivalent heavyweight parser artifacts

scenarios.name: Invalid kind rejected
when: a SymbolNode is created with kind: 'module' (not in the enum)
then: InvalidSymbolKindError is thrown

scenarios.name: Relation direction matters
given: a CALLS relation from symbol A to symbol B
when: callers of symbol B are queried
then: symbol A appears in the results
AND querying callers of symbol A does not return symbol B

scenarios.name: DEPENDS_ON relation connects specs
given: spec A has dependsOn: ['core:config']
when: relations are built from the SpecNode
then: a DEPENDS_ON relation exists from spec A's specId to core:config

scenarios.name: Hierarchy relation types are part of the closed set
when: the relation type set is inspected
then: it includes EXTENDS, IMPLEMENTS, and OVERRIDES
AND adding any additional hierarchy relation type still requires a spec change

scenarios.name: Scoped binding relation types are part of the closed set
when: the relation type set is inspected
then: it includes USES_TYPE and CONSTRUCTS
AND adding any additional scoped binding relation type still requires a spec change

scenarios.name: File-level implementation link becomes COVERS_FILE relation
given: a spec has a file-level archived implementation link to core:src/index.ts
when: graph relations are built from archived traceability
then: a COVERS_FILE relation connects the spec node to that file node

scenarios.name: Symbol-level implementation link becomes COVERS_SYMBOL relation
given: a symbol-level archived implementation link exists for a symbol and spec
when: graph relations are built from archived traceability
then: a COVERS_SYMBOL relation connects the spec node to that symbol node

scenarios.name: Missing symbol marks implementation relation as stale
given: an archived symbol-level implementation link exists
AND the target symbol is no longer detectable in the current graph index
then: the implementation relation is retained
AND its COVERS_SYMBOL metadata marks it as stale: true

scenarios.name: File-level implementation relation is never marked stale
given: an archived file-level implementation link exists for core:src/index.ts
then: the COVERS_FILE relation is retained without stale metadata

scenarios.name: Workspace-boundary materialization failure is not represented as stale
given: an implementation link failed earlier archive-time materialization because of workspace-boundary validation
when: graph relations are built from archived traceability
then: no synthetic stale relation is created for that failure

scenarios.name: Relation equality by source, target, and type
given: two Relation values with the same source, target, and type but different metadata
then: they are considered equal

scenarios.name: Base type inheritance uses EXTENDS
given: a source type inherits from a resolvable base type
then: an EXTENDS relation connects the source type symbol to the base type symbol

scenarios.name: Contract fulfillment uses IMPLEMENTS
given: a source type fulfills a resolvable contract-like declaration
then: an IMPLEMENTS relation connects the source type symbol to the contract symbol

scenarios.name: Method replacement uses OVERRIDES
given: a method deterministically replaces or fulfills a resolvable inherited or contract method
then: an OVERRIDES relation connects the source method symbol to the target method symbol

scenarios.name: Inheritance-adjacent construct that maps cleanly is normalized
given: a supported language exposes an inheritance-adjacent construct that preserves useful impact and hotspot semantics when normalized
then: the construct is represented as one of EXTENDS, IMPLEMENTS, or OVERRIDES

scenarios.name: Inheritance-adjacent construct that does not map cleanly is omitted
given: a supported language exposes a construct whose semantics would be materially distorted by normalization
then: no hierarchy relation is emitted for that construct in this iteration

scenarios.name: Side-effect import has no local symbol
given: a TypeScript file containing import './polyfill.js'
then: the declaration has specifier: './polyfill.js'
AND kind is side-effect
AND localName and originalName are empty strings

scenarios.name: Existing named import remains compatible
given: a TypeScript file containing import { createUser as makeUser } from './user.js'
then: the declaration has localName: 'makeUser'
AND originalName: 'createUser'
AND missing kind is treated as named

scenarios.name: Binding facts are immutable analysis inputs
given: a binding fact for a constructor parameter typed as TemplateExpander
then: it records the workspace-prefixed file path, source location, binding source kind, visible name, and target type candidate
AND its properties are readonly

scenarios.name: Runtime-only binding is outside the model
given: a service lookup identified only by a runtime container string
then: no deterministic binding fact is created for that runtime-only lookup

scenarios.name: Cycle detection breaks infinite parent traversal
given: a malformed scope structure where scope A's parent is B and scope B's parent is A
when: shared binding lookup traverses parent links from scope A
then: the traversal detects the cycle
AND it terminates without entering an infinite loop
AND it returns no resolution result for that lookup

scenarios.name: Constructor call uses CONSTRUCTS relation
given: a function contains new TemplateExpander(builtins)
AND TemplateExpander resolves to a class symbol
when: scoped call resolution emits graph output
then: a CONSTRUCTS relation connects the enclosing function symbol to the TemplateExpander class target

scenarios.name: Constructor injection uses USES_TYPE relation
given: a constructor parameter is typed as TemplateExpander
AND TemplateExpander resolves to a class symbol
when: scoped binding resolution emits graph output
then: a USES_TYPE relation connects the constructor or owning class symbol to the TemplateExpander class target

scenarios.name: Self-relation is not persisted
given: a binding or call fact resolves to the same source and target symbol id
when: scoped binding resolution emits graph output
then: no persisted CALLS, CONSTRUCTS, or USES_TYPE relation is emitted for that self-edge
AND impact analysis does not report the target symbol as its own dependent

scenarios.name: Ambiguous typed dependency emits no speculative edge
given: a parameter type annotation cannot be resolved to a known symbol
then: no persisted relation is emitted for that unresolved type

scenarios.name: FileNode properties are readonly
given: a FileNode instance
when: an attempt is made to reassign contentHash
then: the TypeScript compiler rejects the assignment (compile-time enforcement)

scenarios.name: SpecdCodeGraphError extends SpecdError
then: it extends SpecdError from @specd/core
AND it includes the specd: true discriminator

scenarios.name: Duplicate symbol id detected
given: two symbols in the same file produce the same ID
when: the symbol model validates the file
then: DuplicateSymbolIdError (extending SpecdCodeGraphError) is thrown

scenarios.name: Document identities follow workspace prefixing
when: a document docs/intro.md is indexed in workspace core
then: its identity is core:docs/intro.md

optimizedDescription: Canonical graph model for files, specs, symbols, relations, analysis drafts, and deterministic relation semantics.
optimizedContext:
# Symbol Model

## Rules
- File node: `FileNode` SHALL represent single source file in workspace. It contains: `path` (`string`) — globally unique path in format `{workspaceName}:{relativeToCodeRoot}` (e.g. `core:src/index.ts`). Forward-slash-normalized. This is node's canonical identity. `configRelativePath` (`string`) — forward-slash-normalized path from directory containing `specd.yaml` used for indexing to file on disk (e.g. `packages/core/src/index.ts`). This path is not node identity and MAY include `..` segments when file lives outside config directory. `language` (`string`) — language identifier (e.g. `typescript`, `python`). Determined by language adapter at index time. `contentHash` (`string`) — hash of file's content at last index. Used for incremental diffing. `workspace` (`string`) — workspace name this file belongs to (e.g. `core`, `cli`, `default`). `embedding` (`Float32Array | undefined`) — optional vector embedding for semantic search. Deferred to v2+. Two `FileNode` values are equal if and only if their `path` fields match (since `path` includes workspace name prefix and remains canonical graph identity). `configRelativePath` exists for user-facing lookup and diagnostics; it MUST NOT replace canonical workspace-prefixed path in persisted relations or symbol identifiers.
- Spec node: `SpecNode` SHALL represent spec directory in workspace. It contains: `specId` (`string`) — spec identifier in `workspace:package/topic` format (e.g. `core:change`, `_global:_global/architecture`). This is node's identity. `path` (`string`) — workspace-relative path to spec directory (e.g. `specs/core/change`). `title` (`string`) — from `.specd-metadata.yaml` `title` field. Defaults to `specId` if metadata is absent. `description` (`string`) — from `.specd-metadata.yaml` `description` field. Empty string if absent. `contentHash` (`string`) — hash of concatenated spec artifacts, excluding `.specd-metadata.yaml`. `spec.md` first if present, then remaining artifacts in alphabetical order. Used for incremental diffing. `content` (`string`) — concatenated artifact text for full-text search. Same ordering as `contentHash`: `spec.md` first if present, then remaining artifacts alphabetically. Excludes `.specd-metadata.yaml`. `dependsOn` (`string[]`) — ordered list of spec IDs this spec depends on, extracted from `.specd-metadata.yaml`. Defaults to `[]` if metadata is absent. `workspace` (`string`) — workspace name this spec belongs to (e.g. `core`, `_global`). Two `SpecNode` values are equal if their `specId` fields match.
- Symbol node: `SymbolNode` SHALL represent named code construct extracted from file. It contains: `id` (`string`) — deterministic identifier computed from `filePath + kind + name + line + column` (e.g. `core:src/index.ts:function:main:1:0`). Since `filePath` is workspace-prefixed, id is globally unique across workspaces. same symbol at same location always produces same id. `name` (`string`) — symbol's declared name. `kind` (`SymbolKind`) — category of this symbol. `filePath` (`string`) — workspace-prefixed path of file containing this symbol. `line` (`number`) — 1-based line number of symbol's declaration. `column` (`number`) — 0-based column offset of symbol's declaration. `comment` (`string | undefined`) — raw comment text. All node families (Specs, Files, Symbols, Documents) SHALL use workspace-qualified canonical identities as their unique primary keys.
- File analysis model: code graph SHALL define immutable runtime analysis value objects capture complete compact result of analyzing source file before relation resolution. shared runtime model MUST include: `FileAnalysisDraft` — adapter-emitted file analysis before indexer assigns run-local file identity `FileAnalysis` — registered file analysis includes file's run-local ID, workspace-prefixed path, language, content hash, compact facts, and optional per-file parser state `ParserState` — optional compact parser-specific state retained only when it avoids repeated deterministic work later in same indexing run `AdapterSessionState` — optional compact run-scoped adapter state retained only when it provides deterministic shared caches across multiple files in same indexing run `FileAnalysisDraft` and `FileAnalysis` MUST represent, at minimum: symbols emitted for file import declarations emitted for file deterministic binding facts emitted for file deterministic call facts emitted for file namespace or equivalent qualified-name context when language supports it optional file/document node metadata already normalized for indexing These runtime analysis value objects SHALL remain distinct from persisted graph-store entities. They exist to support in-memory indexing passes and MUST NOT require changes to persisted symbol IDs, relation IDs, or graph-store schemas.
- Shared indexing lookups: symbol model SHALL support run-scoped lookup structures make deterministic relation resolution bounded by relevant matches rather than full symbol scans. shared runtime lookup model MUST support: file lookup by workspace-prefixed path and run-local file ID symbol lookup by file, simple name, and qualified name traversal from file to symbols and symbol back to file lookup of specs and documents participate in cross-entity relations during same indexing run traversal between specs and covered symbols when relationship is needed by later phases relation deduplication keys before final persistence These lookups MAY be implemented by `IndexSession` and `SymbolIndex` collaborating internally, but externally visible runtime model MUST preserve common lookup contract across all built-in adapters and indexer phases.
- Compact retained analysis state: retained runtime analysis model MUST prioritize compact, immutable data over heavyweight parser artifacts to reduce garbage-collection pressure during large indexing runs. Retained `ParserState`, `AdapterSessionState`, binding facts, call facts, imports, and lookup indexes MUST use plain data structures and MUST NOT retain AST nodes, parser trees, parser cursors, or equivalent heavyweight objects once per-file analysis completes.
- Document category: graph SHALL support `document` category for textual non-code nodes. `DocumentNode` represents file does not map to symbol semantics but is searchable via full-text content and identifiable by path. Identities for documents follow same format as `FileNode` (`workspace:path`), including reserved `root:` namespace.
- SymbolKind enum: `SymbolKind` SHALL be closed string enum with following members: `function` `class` `method` `variable` `type` `interface` `enum` No other values are permitted. Language adapters MUST map language-specific constructs to one of these kinds or skip construct entirely.
- Relation types: Relations between nodes SHALL be represented as typed edges. Each relation has `source`, `target`, and `type`. following relation types are defined: | Type | Source | Target | Meaning | Phase | | --------------| -----| -----| --------------------------------------------------------------------| ----| | `IMPORTS` | File | File | Source file imports from target file | v1.5 | | `DEFINES` | File | Symbol | File contains symbol's declaration | v2 | | `CALLS` | Symbol | Symbol | Source symbol invokes target symbol | v2 | | `CONSTRUCTS` | Symbol | Symbol | Source symbol constructs or instantiates target type | v2 | | `USES_TYPE` | Symbol | Symbol | Source symbol references target type in static signature or binding | v2 | | `EXPORTS` | File | Symbol | File exports symbol as public API | v2 | | `DEPENDS_ON` | Spec | Spec | Spec depends on target spec | v1.5 | | `COVERS_FILE` | Spec | File | Spec has file-level implementation link to target file | v2+ | | `COVERS_SYMBOL` | Spec | Symbol | Spec has symbol-level implementation link to target symbol | v2+ | | `EXTENDS` | Symbol | Symbol | Source type extends or inherits from target type | v2 | | `IMPLEMENTS` | Symbol | Symbol | Source type fulfills or implements target contract/type | v2 | | `OVERRIDES` | Symbol | Symbol | Source method overrides or concretely fulfills target one | v2 |
- Relation staleness: Relations MAY include `stale` boolean flag in their `metadata`. For implementation traceability, `stale` applies specifically to `COVERS_SYMBOL` relations whose target symbol is no longer detectable in current graph index. It is not generic marker for workspace-boundary or archive-materialization failures.
- Relation value object: `Relation` SHALL be value object containing: `source` (`string`) — id or path of source node. `target` (`string`) — id or path of target node. `type` (`RelationType`) — one of relation types defined above. `metadata` (`Record<string, unknown> | undefined`) — optional adapter-specific metadata (e.g. import specifier, call site line number). Two `Relation` values are equal if `source`, `target`, and `type` all match.
- Hierarchy relation semantics: Hierarchy relations SHALL use following normalization rules: `EXTENDS` connects one type symbol to another type symbol when source inherits behavior or structure from target. `IMPLEMENTS` connects one type symbol to another contract-bearing symbol when source fulfills target's required shape or behavior. `OVERRIDES` connects one method symbol to another method symbol when source method replaces, concretizes, or fulfills target method through inheritance or contract implementation. `OVERRIDES` is first-class persisted relation in this model; it is not deferred to derived traversal logic. Adapters MAY normalize inheritance-adjacent constructs from supported languages into these relations when normalized edge remains useful for code understanding, impact discovery, hotspot analysis, and affected-spec discovery.
- Import declaration: `ImportDeclaration` SHALL represent parsed import statement or deterministic import expression extracted from source code. It contains: `localName` (`string`) — identifier used locally in importing file. File-only import forms use empty string. `originalName` (`string`) — identifier as declared in source module. File-only import forms use empty string. `specifier` (`string`) — raw import specifier string. `isRelative` (`boolean`) — true if specifier is relative path. `kind` (`'named' | 'namespace' | 'default' | 'side-effect' | 'dynamic' | 'require' | 'blank' | undefined`) — optional import form metadata. `undefined` is equivalent to `named` for backwards compatibility. `ImportDeclaration` is pure syntactic representation — it contains no resolution information. indexer resolves specifiers to files and symbol ids using adapter registry and monorepo package map. Side-effect imports, string-literal dynamic imports, CommonJS `require()` calls, and Go blank imports MUST be representable as file-only import declarations without creating fake local symbols.
- Scoped binding model: symbol model SHALL define immutable, language-agnostic value objects for deterministic scoped binding and call resolution. These value objects are analysis inputs and intermediate results normalize source-language facts before they are converted into persisted graph relations. shared model SHALL represent: Scope ownership: file, class/type, method, function, and block scopes when adapter can identify them deterministically. Binding facts: source-location-aware facts bind visible name or receiver to declared, constructed, imported, inherited, framework-managed, or otherwise deterministic target type or symbol candidate. Binding source kind: local, parameter, property/field, class-managed, inherited, file/global, imported type, framework-managed, constructor call, and alias-from-known-binding. Call facts: source-location-aware facts for free calls, member calls, static calls, and constructor calls, including enclosing caller symbol when known. Resolution metadata: deterministic reason strings and optional confidence or diagnostic metadata explain why binding or call candidate was accepted or dropped. Binding facts and call facts MUST be immutable value objects with `readonly` properties. They MUST use workspace-prefixed file paths and symbol IDs when they reference graph entities. Cycle Detection: scoped binding environment resolution SHALL implement cycle detection when traversing scope hierarchy (e.g., following `parentId` links). If circular reference is detected, traversal MUST be aborted to prevent infinite loops, and no resolution result SHALL be returned for lookup. first iteration SHALL remain conservative. It MUST NOT model arbitrary runtime state, reflection, runtime container identifiers, interprocedural propagation, or whole-program data flow.
- Scoped binding relation output: Scoped binding and call resolution SHALL emit deterministic graph relations using persisted relation vocabulary: `IMPORTS` for file-level dependencies resolve to files. `CALLS` for deterministic invocations from one symbol to another callable symbol. `CONSTRUCTS` for deterministic constructor or constructor-like instantiation from enclosing symbol to constructed class/type symbol. `USES_TYPE` for deterministic type references from enclosing symbol to referenced type, interface, enum, or class symbol. `EXTENDS`, `IMPLEMENTS`, and `OVERRIDES` for deterministic hierarchy and method-fulfillment relationships. Constructor injection, typed parameters, typed return values, typed properties/fields, and imported type annotations SHALL emit `USES_TYPE` when referenced type symbol resolves deterministically. Constructor calls such as `new ClassName()` and language-equivalent constructor-like expressions SHALL emit `CONSTRUCTS` when constructed target resolves deterministically. Scoped binding and call resolution SHALL NOT persist symbol-to-symbol dependency relations where source symbol id and target symbol id are identical. Self-relations do not represent external blast radius and MUST be dropped before graph persistence. Unresolved binding facts and call facts SHALL NOT create speculative graph edges. They MAY be preserved as non-persisted diagnostics or debug metadata for tests and troubleshooting.
- Immutability: All model types — `FileNode`, `SymbolNode`, `Relation`, and any aggregate containing them — SHALL be immutable value objects. Properties MUST be `readonly`. Mutations to graph happen exclusively through `GraphStore` port's atomic upsert operations, replace entire file-scoped slices. There is no in-place mutation API.
- Error types: `@specd/code-graph` SHALL define its own `SpecdCodeGraphError` base class MUST extend `SpecdError` from `@specd/core`. All package-specific errors MUST extend `SpecdCodeGraphError`. By extending `SpecdError`, all code-graph errors automatically follow "Specd Error Contract" defined in `default:_global/error-handling-conventions`, including `specd: true` discriminator and machine-readable `code` requirement. `InvalidSymbolKindError` — when symbol kind value is not in `SymbolKind` enum `InvalidRelationTypeError` — when relation type value is not in defined set `DuplicateSymbolIdError` — when two symbols in same file produce same deterministic id `StoreNotOpenError` — when operation is attempted on closed or uninitialized store

## Constraints
- All model types are value objects — no identity beyond structural equality (except SymbolNode.id is deterministic)
- SymbolKind is closed enum — adding new kind requires spec change
- RelationType is closed set — adding new type requires spec change
- Depends on @specd/core as runtime dependency
- embedding on FileNode is deferred to v2+ — adapters MUST NOT populate it until embedding pipeline is implemented
- COVERS relations are deferred to v2+ — type exists in model but is not populated by any v1.5/v2 process
- DEPENDS_ON relations are populated by indexer in v1.5, reading from .specd-metadata.yaml or spec.md
- CONSTRUCTS and USES_TYPE are first-class persisted relations in v2 and are part of base graph vocabulary for supported languages
- EXTENDS, IMPLEMENTS, and OVERRIDES are first-class persisted relations in v2 and are part of base graph vocabulary for supported languages
